step_by_step_thinking:
\boxed{C}
**Clinical Reasoning:** Placebo refers to a sham intervention that lacks any active therapeutic component but produces beneficial effects due to patient expectation. Among the listed options, sham surgery mimics real surgical procedure without actual tissue manipulation, thus representing a well-documented form of placebo effect where subjective improvement occurs despite absence of biological change. In contrast, herbal medications, physiotherapy, and cognitive-behavioral therapy involve active treatments with measurable physiological or psychological impacts.
